title: Intensely Creamy Spanish Gruyere Tapas Platter
description: A rich and creamy Catalan-inspired tapas dish featuring Gruyere cheese, perfect for a quick and satisfying snack. This gluten-free friendly recipe is made with pressure-cooked ingredients for a fast and delicious meal.
instructions: In a pressure cooker, combine the heavy cream, melted butter, garlic, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
While the cream mixture is cooking, prepare the Gruyere cheese by shredding it into a bowl.
Once the cream mixture is done, stir in the shredded Gruyere cheese until it is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes to the cheese mixture and stir gently to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a serving platter or individual tapas dishes.
Garnish with additional smoked paprika and chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
To make ahead, prepare the cream mixture and cheese mixture separately, then combine just before serving.
